title: Slow Braised Thai Pork Curry
description: A rich and flavorful Thai curry made with pork, clear honey, and a blend of spices, perfect for a lazy Sunday dinner. This dish is sugar-free-friendly and features a fatty and silky texture.
instructions: Heat the oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the pork and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the curry paste and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the honey, fish sauce, and lime juice, and stir to combine.
Pour in the coconut milk and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 2 1/2 hours, or until the pork is tender.
Stir in the chopped basil and season with salt to taste.
Serve the curry over rice or noodles, garnished with additional basil if desired.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
